title: Erratic driver in front-end loader leads police on 10- to 15-mph chase through Mass. town
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Off Beat The driver nearly struck multiple utility poles and swerved into oncoming traffic, police said.
A driver in a front-end loader led police on a low-speed chase in Millbury Saturday night.
Millbury Police In a scene sure to have raised eyebrows among nearby drivers, police in a Worcester suburb engaged in an extremely low-speed chase Saturday involving a front-end loader.

title: S&S Deli, a Cambridge landmark, to close after 107 years
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Restaurants The Inman Square institution is ending its century-long run serving pastrami, knishes, matzo ball soup, and other comfort-food staples.
A photo of Ma Edelstein, the founder of S&S Deli, hangs behind the cash register of the restaurant in 2005.
Wendy Maeda / The Boston Globe, File ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Police searching for girl, 13, missing from Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Mineidys Soto-Lugo is described as Hispanic, approximately 5'2" tall, with a medium build, brown eyes, and brown shoulder-length hair with a blond streak.
Mineidys Soto-Lugo, of Dorchester.
Courtesy of Boston Police Department ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Mass. residents fighting to keep TPS for Haitians, Syrians await SCOTUS decision
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Politics Advocates and experts warn the ruling could reshape protections for more than 45,000 Haitians in Massachusetts and over 1.
3 million TPS holders nationwide.
Members of the National TPS Alliance rallied at the Supreme Court in Washington, on Wednesday.

title: For $739k, live at shuttered Sailor Stan’s restaurant in Gloucester
author: Regina Cole
bullets: On the Market Since 1977, Sailor Stan's operated on Rocky Neck.
Now, the restaurant and adjoining three-bedroom residence is for sale.
Sailor Stan's in Rocky Neck opened in 1977 and closed a couple years ago.

title: Amherst police investigating ‘hateful and racist letters’ left on residents’ vehicles
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime “These statements are deeply upsetting and do not reflect the values of our Town, which are built on principles of inclusion and respect,” town officials said.
Amherst police are investigating several “hateful and racist” letters found on residents’ cars last week that were signed by a cryptic organization, the town said.
“Harm has been felt through the community, and it will not be ignored,” the town of Amherst said in a statement posted on Facebook.

title: Red Sox starter Ranger Suarez exits game early due to hamstring issue
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "They’re going to keep him back with our medical people and we’ll see in the next couple days where we’re at.
” Ranger Suarez was pulled after four scoreless innings of work on Sunday.
AP Photo/Mark Stockwell By Conor Ryan May 3, 2026 | 7:48 PM 2 minutes to read The hits keep on coming for the Red Sox’ starting rotation.

title: Bruins’ plans for captain and 4 other takeaways from Boston’s breakup day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "We definitely expect those guys upstairs to bring us some help as well and push even harder for the Stanley Cup.
” Jeremy Swayman and the Bruins packed up their bags on Sunday.

Ken McGagh for The Boston Globe By Conor Ryan May 3, 2026 | 7:19 PM 9 minutes to read Just two days after the Bruins’ 2025-26 season came to a close, Boston’s players, coaches, and staff were on hand at Warrior Ice Arena Sunday to debrief, pack up their things, and prepare for a busy offseason.

title: Death of baby in Taunton under investigation
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime The 8-month-old baby boy was found unresponsive at a home in Taunton, prosecutors said.
By Carson Lyle May 3, 2026 | 6:43 PM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Police are investigating after an infant was found unresponsive in a Taunton home and later died, the local DA said.

title: 7 takeaways from the Celtics’ end-of-the-season press conferences
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Jayson Tatum discussed his injury status as the Celtics brought this season to a close.
Jayson Tatum addressed the media, a day after the Celtics' Game 7 loss to the 76ers.
